title: Dactylicapnos lichiangensis
intro: Dactylicapnos lichiangensis, also known as the Yunnan poppy or Chinese poppy, is a flowering plant species in the family Papaveraceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its purported health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various health conditions, such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Dactylicapnos lichiangensis include alkaloids, terpenoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Dactylicapnos lichiangensis is often used to make herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory problems and fever.

The species is further classified as a genus within the Papaveraceae family. Dactylicapnos lichiangensis is a unique species that is closely related to other members of the Papaveraceae family. It is often grouped together with other species within the same family due to its shared characteristics.
uses: Dactylicapnos lichiangensis, also known as the Himalayan bleeding heart, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes.
